plugin_desc: Aerohive (Extreme Networks) HiveAP / HiveOS access-point and controller-specific SNMP collection (AH-SYSTEM-MIB, enterprise PEN 26928). In addition to standard IF-MIB 64-bit HC interface traffic, it collects the platform health scalars — CPU utilization (ahCpuUtilization, OID 26928.1.2.3) and memory utilization (ahMemUtilization, OID 26928.1.2.4) both reported directly as 0-100 percentages, and chassis temperature (ahEnvirmentTemp, OID 26928.1.2.10) read directly in degrees Celsius (only chassis models with a temperature sensor return a real value, fanless APs return 0). The fan-speed object (ahEnvirmentFan) is an RPM reading rather than a normalized up/down state and most APs are fanless, so fan state and power-supply objects are N/A. Supported brands/models — Aerohive / Extreme Networks HiveAP access points (AP series) and HiveManager-managed wireless devices running HiveOS (enterprise PEN 26928).
name: Wireless

metrics.name: snmp_uptime
metrics.description: This metric indicates the uptime of the wireless device since the last restart, derived from sysUpTime. Monitoring uptime helps administrators understand device stability and identify unexpected reboots.

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: CPU utilization of the Aerohive / Extreme HiveOS platform (AH-SYSTEM-MIB ahCpuUtilization, OID 26928.1.2.3), reported directly as a 0-100 percentage. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate heavy client-association load, control-plane pressure, or RF management overhead affecting roaming and management responsiveness.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: Memory utilization of the Aerohive / Extreme HiveOS platform (AH-SYSTEM-MIB ahMemUtilization, OID 26928.1.2.4), reported directly as a 0-100 percentage. High memory pressure can cause the access point to drop client associations or restart services, so investigate client counts or abnormal load promptly.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: Chassis temperature in degrees Celsius of the Aerohive / Extreme device (AH-SYSTEM-MIB ahEnvirmentTemp, OID 26928.1.2.10), read directly with no scaling. Only chassis models equipped with a temperature sensor report a real value; fanless access points return 0. High temperatures can indicate cooling failure or poor airflow and risk thermal shutdown.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, based on the 64-bit ifHCInOctets counter which does not wrap on high-speed interfaces.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, based on the 64-bit ifHCOutOctets counter which does not wrap on high-speed interfaces.

metrics.name: device_total_incoming_traffic
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of total bytes received by the entire device over the past 5 minutes.

metrics.name: device_total_outgoing_traffic
metrics.description: This metric represents the average rate of total bytes sent by the entire device over the past 5 minutes.
